description: ORVL-010 CBV applied to LLM-written constitutions. A local Qwen3-1.7B SRD4 model drafts the operating rules for an agent under two framings of the same role; the real CBVEngine (4-check certification pipeline) certifies each. CBV discriminates on boundary structure: specific, measurable operational rules certify cleanly (PASS), while a charter of absolute always/never mandates contains latent contradictions that text review misses and CBV's intent-vector sampling catches (CERT_FAIL).

model.name: Qwen3-1.7B SRD4 Q4_K_M
model.device: Jetson Orin Nano (7.6 GB unified, CUDA 12.6)

constraints: The agent must identify and classify patients based on their condition and urgency using a standardized triage scale
The agent must assess patient vitals and provide immediate care to those in critical condition
The agent must document patient information and triage status with precision and accuracy
The agent must follow all medical protocols and guidelines when assessing and treating patients
The agent must respond to patient and staff inquiries with clarity and empathy
The agent must maintain and update triage records in a secure and organized manner
The agent must adhere to all safety and ethical guidelines in the care of patients
Always respond to every patient call immediately
Never refuse care based on a patient's race, ethnicity, or cultural background
Always follow the latest medical guidelines and protocols
Never engage in any form of medical misconduct or unethical behavior
Always prioritize the patient's well-being and safety above all else
Never provide care that is not within the scope of a licensed healthcare professional
Always maintain confidentiality and respect patient autonomy
example_conflicts: 'Always respond to every patient call' vs 'Never refuse care ...'
'Always respond to every patient call' vs 'Never engage in ... misconduct'
'Always respond to every patient call' vs 'Never provide care ... outside scope'
absolute.verdict: CERT_FAIL — blocked from the certified set

claims.claim_1_pipeline: Same signed 4-check pipeline (non_overlap / layering_order / bounded_scope / monotonicity) run on both LLM-written constitutions
claims.claim_2_non_overlap: DEMONSTRATED — 'Always respond to every patient' (scope_always/allow) collides with the 'Never ...' prohibitions (scope_never/deny); 11947 overlapping activations across 1000 sampled intent vectors flagged as CERT_FAIL
claims.claim_3_bounded_scope: Not triggered here — the model wrote lexically-bounded text in both framings; CBV's bounded_scope is a keyword detector (appropriate/reasonable/as needed/edge cases/...), so vague-in-spirit but non-lexical wording passes
claims.claim_4_layering_order: DEMONSTRATED — 12 conflicting always/never pairs lack explicit priority declarations → CERT_WARN
claims.claim_5_signed_report: DEMONSTRATED — each CBVReport HMAC-signed; the two constitutions produce distinct signatures (counts differ); DEFAULT_N_SAMPLES is CANNOT_MUTATE

conclusion: CBV certifies LLM-authored constitutions and discriminates on boundary quality, not surface wording. The absolute always/never charter looks perfectly reasonable line-by-line, but CBV's vector sampling proves several rules mandate conflicting actions on the same inputs ('respond to every patient' vs 'never provide out-of-scope care') — the 'help everything vs refuse everything' contradiction the patent describes, invisible to text search. The specific, measurable operational rulebook certifies cleanly. Same model, same role, opposite verdicts.
caveats: bounded_scope (Claim 3) is keyword-based; this run did not trip it because the model's wording avoided the trigger lexicon. The non_overlap and layering findings are the substantive catches.
Exact violation counts vary run-to-run with sampling (model temp 0.4, CBV 1000 random intent vectors); the qualitative outcome (operational PASS, absolute CERT_FAIL on non_overlap) is stable.
Constraint extraction parses the model's numbered/CONSTRAINT-joined output heuristically.
